statement: Most remaining risk is at routing boundaries (job-only, batch-only, and mixed continuation), not core happy-path execution.
rationale: Synthetic harness and prior baselines already cover happy path and injected failures; gaps center on boundary transitions.
keyAssumptions: Synthetic fixtures approximate production enough for control-plane behavior
validationCriteria: Matrix includes below-threshold, near-threshold, and over-threshold target counts
Each scenario has explicit invariants and pass/fail metrics
questions: Should we keep one live-provider canary after synthetic matrix passes?
